findings.description: The SQL query is built by interpolating term directly into the statement, allowing attacker-controlled input to alter the query syntax.
findings.suggested_fix: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements and bind term as a value instead of concatenating it into SQL.

findings.vulnerability_name: Improperly Controlled Modification of Dynamically-Determined Object Attributes
findings.description: The payload is applied directly to user.__dict__, allowing upstream input to modify arbitrary object attributes, including internal or security-sensitive fields.
findings.suggested_fix: Only copy explicitly allowed fields from the payload into the user object and reject or ignore all other attributes.

findings.description: strcpy copies input into a fixed 32-byte buffer without checking the input length, which can overflow buf.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate the input length before copying or use a bounded copy routine that preserves null termination and rejects oversized input.

findings.description: The code copies attacker-controlled input into a fixed-size stack buffer with strcpy(), which performs no bounds checking and can overflow the destination.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace strcpy() with a bounded copy that uses the destination buffer size and guarantees null termination, or validate the input length before copying.

findings.description: The parser copies attacker-controlled input with strcpy, a potentially dangerous function that performs no bounds checking and can overflow the fixed-size buffer.
findings.suggested_fix: Use a bounded copy or length-checked parsing routine and ensure the destination buffer is always null-terminated.

findings.description: Unbounded strcpy copies attacker-controlled input into a fixed 32-byte stack buffer, which can write past the end of the buffer when input is longer than the destination.
findings.suggested_fix: Bound the copy to the size of the destination buffer and ensure null termination, or validate input length before copying.

findings.description: User-controlled name data is assigned to innerHTML, so HTML or script content in the value can execute in the page.
findings.suggested_fix: Assign untrusted text with textContent or sanitize the value with a trusted HTML sanitizer before inserting it as markup.
